selected_commands.name: ultrathink
selected_commands.savage_assessment: More abstraction layers than a wedding cake at a software architecture conference. One point away from violating the sacred < 5 rule.

selected_commands.name: java-clean-code-generator
selected_commands.savage_assessment: A command that preaches simplicity while being complex itself - like a diet book written by someone who's never seen a salad.

selected_commands.name: adhd-morning-assistant
selected_commands.savage_assessment: Finally! A command that respects the complexity threshold. Sophisticated without being pretentious. This is what intelligence looks like.

selected_commands.name: safe-code-beautifier
selected_commands.savage_assessment: A beautifier that's almost as complex as ugly code itself. Makes me wonder if we need a beautifier for the beautifier.

selected_commands.name: adaptive-complexity-router
selected_commands.savage_assessment: Routes complexity by being complex - like solving traffic jams by adding more roads. The most useful over-engineered thing since sliced bread servers.

selected_commands.name: senior-developer-analysis
selected_commands.savage_assessment: Analyzes itself into near-complexity violation. Like inception for code review. Complexity matches the problem domain though.

savage_commentary.summary: These commands are like sports cars - impressive engineering, high performance, but you better know how to drive them.
the_good: All commands respect the sacred < 5 complexity threshold
ADHD Morning Assistant shows how to be sophisticated without being pretentious
Thinking orchestration patterns are consistently well-structured
the_bad: 67% of commands are in the danger zone (≥4 complexity)
Token consumption is astronomical - 10K+ tokens regularly
Irony of complex commands preaching simplicity is thicker than molasses
the_ugly: More abstraction layers than a software architecture textbook
Adaptive Complexity Router needs its own complexity router
One design pattern away from violating our own rules
savage_commentary.recommendation: Use with caution, monitor token consumption, consider creating 'diet' versions for simple tasks

best_command.name: adhd-morning-assistant
best_command.reason: Best balance of sophistication and simplicity, highest predicted success rate

worst_command.name: adaptive-complexity-router
worst_command.reason: Highest complexity with lowest success rate, ironic over-engineering

most_ironic.name: java-clean-code-generator
most_ironic.reason: Preaches simplicity while being the second most complex command
